meaning: 釜石[日本本州岛东北岸港市]
examples: Rain or sun, Mr Shimokawara sticks to his routine, at the municipal stadium or around his home town of Kamaishi, northeastern Japan.
Longevity in Japan is notably more pronounced in the countryside and in small towns such as Kamaishi, where the pace of life is less stressful than in the metropolis.
When the Great East Japan Earthquake and Tsunami struck on 11 March 2011, almost all the elementary and junior high school children in the small coastal town of Kamaishi survived.
Across Kamaishi, reconstruction is happening slowly.
